summary: Loader package executes unrelated sibling dependency on require()
details: @sql-access/nodesql@1.0.8 presents itself as three different things: the package name suggests a SQL access library, the README describes a streaming library called 'bare-stream', and the actual index.js is a verbatim copy of the well-known feross/buffer module. A single line has been injected at the top of index.js (`var ins = require('@sqlite-node/createsql');`) where the variable `ins` is never read anywhere else in the file. The require runs purely for its side effects — any consumer that imports @sql-access/nodesql will execute the top-level code of @sqlite-node/createsql at import time. The dependency name has no relationship to either Buffer or to streams, and the metadata-vs-code mismatch is consistent with disguising the injected import to reduce reviewer scrutiny. The actual payload is delivered by the sibling package; this package is the loader edge that pulls it into the installer's tree and triggers it.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
